items.reason: Every entry in `docs/decisions/decisions-needed.md` (DEC-001 through DEC-020, including the new platform-shape decisions) has a corresponding decision in `docs/decisions/standards-overlap-decisions.md` with id, question, options_considered (three options each), choice, consequences, and projects_to lists. DEC-016 through DEC-020 explicitly cover dictionary direction, canonical fields, global enums, closed privacy classes, and migration generation.

`docs/decisions/decisions-pending.md` lists PEND-001-runtime-slice-schedule (owner Andy, target 2026-05-15, blocker 'Product priority on next runtime slice', rationale present) and PEND-005-deno-check-in-ci (owner Andy, target 2026-05-03, blocker 'CI capacity'). Both have all required fields and target dates in the future.

Most decisions (DEC-001 through DEC-016) have at least one consequence bullet starting with 'Removes the need for...' or 'Eliminates...' naming concrete artifacts (e.g., DEC-010 removes per-table tenant filtering). However, DEC-017 through DEC-020 consequences mostly add requirements (canonical_field_id, shared_enum_id, closed list, dictionary graph) without naming the existing artifact or code path being deleted (e.g., 'removes adapter X', 'collapses generator Y'). The substance_check explicitly flags this 'adds requirements without naming what it removes' pattern as partial.
items.traced_cause: docs/decisions/standards-overlap-decisions.md DEC-017 through DEC-020: rewrite consequences to name a concrete artifact, code path, or category each decision deletes (e.g., DEC-018 should name the per-spec enum tables/parsers that are collapsed; DEC-020 should name the hand-written `supabase/migrations/0001_oneroster_core_demo.sql` blocks that the generator replaces).

items.reason: Each decision lists three options including more-aggressive and more-permissive alternatives. Most are credible (DEC-005's 'use AGS as canonical gradebook' is a real choice some platforms make). However, several decisions still pair an obvious-bookend choice against the chosen middle: DEC-014's 'delete all static mirrors' vs 'treat as canonical' vs the chosen middle reads as a manufactured spread, and DEC-001's 'separate table per standard' is a strawman (no production identity platform proposes that). The substance_check forbids 'two extreme bookends plus a chosen middle, where the bookends are obviously wrong.'
items.traced_cause: docs/decisions/standards-overlap-decisions.md DEC-001 and DEC-014: replace the strawman bookend options with credible architectural choices a competent architect would actually consider (e.g., for DEC-001 replace 'separate table per standard' with 'identity-as-graph with confidence-weighted edges', as Salesforce/HubSpot-style identity stitching does).

items.reason: No `scripts/generate_spec_dictionaries.py` exists in the evidence. The per-spec dictionaries (`dictionary/oneroster-core.v1.json`, `dictionary/qti-core.v1.json`, etc.) are clearly hand-maintained — they are the upstream files read directly by `scripts/generate_oneroster_core.py`, `scripts/generate_qti_core.py`, etc. The `data/data-dictionary.seed.json` referenced in DEC-016 is not present in evidence and the loop-overrides describe it as a 21-field stub against 568 per-spec fields. DEC-016 was decided but not implemented.
items.traced_cause: data/data-dictionary.seed.json: expand into a canonical model with every object, field, enum, privacy class, and relationship currently in the per-spec dictionaries; then write `scripts/generate_spec_dictionaries.py` that reads it and generates `dictionary/*.v1.json` deterministically; add a CI check that fails if generator output diverges.

items.reason: Per-spec fields do not carry `canonical_field_id`. For example, `dictionary/oneroster-core.v1.json#person.email` has only `decision_id` and `sourceStandard`, no canonical reference. `dictionary/caliper-core.v1.json#caliper_actor.identifier_value` and `dictionary/integration-governance-core.v1.json#lti_membership.email` (visible in oauth_scope_policies field references) likewise lack any `canonical_field_id`. The substance_check requires the four overlapping email fields (OneRoster, Caliper, LTI, QTI Candidate) to all point at one shared canonical email — none of them do.
items.traced_cause: dictionary/oneroster-core.v1.json#person.email, dictionary/caliper-core.v1.json#caliper_actor.identifier_value, dictionary/integration-governance-core.v1.json#lti_membership.email: add `canonical_field_id` referring to a shared `data/data-dictionary.seed.json#person.email` (or equivalent canonical identity field). DEC-017 names this as the required projection but the data has not been added.

items.reason: Identity-shaped objects across specs do not carry `canonical_object_id`. `dictionary/oneroster-core.v1.json#person`, `dictionary/caliper-core.v1.json#caliper_actor`, and `dictionary/integration-governance-core.v1.json#lti_membership` have only `object_key`, `name`, and `sourceStandard`. There is no canonical identity entity record in the shared dictionary (the shared dictionary is a stub). DEC-001 unifies identity in prose only.
items.traced_cause: data/data-dictionary.seed.json: add a canonical `person` object with fields, allowed values, and projections; then add `canonical_object_id: 'person'` to `dictionary/oneroster-core.v1.json#person`, `dictionary/caliper-core.v1.json#caliper_actor`, `dictionary/integration-governance-core.v1.json#lti_membership`, and the QTI Candidate object.

items.reason: Per-spec enums are declared independently rather than referencing a shared enum. `dictionary/oneroster-core.v1.json#person.primary_role` lists allowed_values inline (student/teacher/administrator/guardian) with `sourceStandard` pointing back to OneRoster — no `shared_enum_id`. `dictionary/integration-governance-core.v1.json#lti_launch.roles` and `dictionary/integration-governance-core.v1.json#lti_membership.roles` similarly have their own value lists. There is no bidirectional crosswalk to a shared `role_family` enum. DEC-018 was decided but not projected into data.
items.traced_cause: data/data-dictionary.seed.json: add a `shared_enums.role_family` table with canonical role-family values and bidirectional crosswalks; replace `allowed_values` blocks in `dictionary/oneroster-core.v1.json#person.primary_role`, `dictionary/oneroster-core.v1.json#enrollment.role`, and `dictionary/integration-governance-core.v1.json#lti_launch.roles` with `shared_enum_id: 'role_family'`.

items.reason: `dictionary/caliper-core.v1.json` uses the forbidden escape-hatch values: `caliper_entity.name` has `"privacy_class": "depends_on_entity"`, `caliper_entity.description` has `"depends_on_entity"`, and `caliper_entity.extensions` has `"depends_on_contents"`. DEC-019 explicitly forbids these placeholders and lists the closed set, but the data still contains them. Loop-overrides also explicitly call this out as required cleanup.
items.traced_cause: dictionary/caliper-core.v1.json#caliper_entity.name, .description, and .extensions: replace `depends_on_entity` and `depends_on_contents` with concrete privacy classes from DEC-019's closed list (e.g., resolve `name` to `directory` or `education_record` per occurrence; resolve `extensions` to `behavioral` or `sensitive` based on the actual extension key contracts).

items.reason: `supabase/migrations/0001_oneroster_core_demo.sql` is hand-written (479 lines of CREATE TABLE, FK, and RLS policy SQL) and not generated from any dictionary relational graph. The per-spec dictionary objects (e.g., `dictionary/oneroster-core.v1.json#enrollment`) do not carry a `relationships` block declaring FK targets and cardinality. DEC-020 decided this should change but no generator exists and no relational graph data has been added.
items.traced_cause: data/data-dictionary.seed.json and dictionary/*.v1.json objects: add a `relationships` array per object declaring fk_target, cardinality, and ownership; then write `scripts/generate_supabase_migrations.py` that emits `supabase/migrations/0001_oneroster_core_demo.sql` deterministically from that graph.

items.reason: Every field, object, and enum value visible in evidence carries a `decision_id`. `dictionary/oneroster-core.v1.json#person.email` has `decision_id: 'DEC-001-person-agent-subject'`; `caliper_event.event_time` has `'DEC-008-time-session'`; objects carry decision references in their sourceStandard projections via `scripts/check_dictionary_artifacts.py` which cross-checks every dictionary field against `docs/decisions/standards-overlap-decisions.md` decision-trace markers and fails on broken links.

Generated dictionary pages do exist: `docs/generated/oneroster-core-dictionary.md`, `docs/generated/caliper-core-dictionary.md`, etc., and `scripts/build_site_docs.py` renders them. However, load-bearing prose pages do not link to dictionary anchors for every claim: `docs/lead-spec-accounting.md` lists hundreds of fields like `Demographics.birthDate` and `User.userMasterIdentifier` as plain text with no anchor links into the dictionary, and `docs/dictionary-coverage-matrix.md` describes coverage in prose without anchored field references. The substance_check requires every load-bearing claim to link to a dictionary anchor.
items.traced_cause: docs/lead-spec-accounting.md and docs/dictionary-coverage-matrix.md: convert each named field reference to a link into the corresponding `docs/generated/*-dictionary.md#field-anchor`, or move the prose content to a generated page so the dictionary is the single source.

items.reason: The decision register `docs/decisions/standards-overlap-decisions.md` exists and renders to HTML at `site/docs/decisions-standards-overlap-decisions.html`, but no doc page outside the register itself explains each decision's choice and simplification in plain language. The portal `site/index.html` decisions section pulls cards from the register but the substance_check explicitly fails 'a link to a 900-line raw Markdown file as the only explanation'. There is no per-decision narrative page (e.g., 'why person and agent are different') in `docs/`.
items.traced_cause: docs/why/<decision-slug>.md: write a per-decision plain-language page for each DEC-001 through DEC-020 explaining the choice and the work it deletes, link from the portal navigation, and reference the decision register only for the formal record.

items.reason: `docs/build-an-edtech-app.md` does not exist in the evidence. The site/docs/index.html catalog of rendered pages lists the decision register, dictionary pages, coverage matrix, and feedback package — but no end-to-end buildability guide. The five required steps (create school, enroll student in class, post grade, link CASE standard, read Caliper feed) have no copy-pasteable curl/SQL walkthrough anywhere in the docs.
items.traced_cause: docs/build-an-edtech-app.md: write the five-step guide with copy-pasteable curl commands against the live Supabase REST URL and Edge Functions (`https://qzxlgrerjoiamxvnkklq.supabase.co/rest/v1/...` and `https://qzxlgrerjoiamxvnkklq.functions.supabase.co/...`), with every field reference linking to its dictionary anchor and every enum value linking to the global enum.

items.reason: The docs index `site/docs/index.html` reaches 25 generated/rendered pages and the portal `site/index.html` links to many of them. However, no link checker output is present in evidence, and `site/index.html` references `docs/generated-oneroster-core-dictionary.html` (which the build_site_docs renames using `-` separators) — the actual file at `site/docs/oneroster-core-dictionary.html` may or may not be aliased. Additionally, the spec-gap-backlog item #25 explicitly notes 'duplicate or orphaned HTML' from generators emitting both direct HTML and slugged Markdown-rendered HTML.
items.traced_cause: scripts/build_site_docs.py and site/index.html: run a link checker over the rendered site, normalize the dual generated-dictionary URL pattern (direct `site/docs/oneroster-core-dictionary.html` vs. slugged `site/docs/generated-oneroster-core-dictionary.html`), and resolve the orphans called out in spec-gap-backlog.md item 25.

items.reason: A layperson reading only `docs/` cannot produce executable curl/SQL for the five required steps. There is no `docs/build-an-edtech-app.md`. Even if the layperson finds the Supabase REST URL in `docs/supabase-hosted-database.md`, that file shows only a single `select=id,display_name` smoke curl — it does not show how to create a school, create a class, enroll students, post a grade, link a CASE standard, or read a Caliper feed. The dictionary docs describe field shapes but do not assemble them into a workflow. The layperson would also hit unresolved cross-spec overlaps (no canonical email field, no shared role enum), forcing them to read every spec themselves.
items.traced_cause: docs/build-an-edtech-app.md is missing AND the underlying dictionary lacks the canonical_field_id/canonical_object_id/shared_enum_id projections needed for the guide to reference one canonical email/role/identity. Both must change: write the guide AND add the canonical projections it depends on.

items.reason: This evaluation includes a `traced_cause` field on every non-pass item naming the lowest-layer artifact and required change (e.g., 'data/data-dictionary.seed.json: add canonical person object'; 'dictionary/caliper-core.v1.json#caliper_entity.name: replace depends_on_entity with concrete privacy class'). `scripts/evaluate_platform.py` enforces this via its `ensure_traced_causes` helper which fills in defaults from `DEFAULT_TRACED_CAUSES` if the LLM omits one.

`VERIFY.md` ends with `python3 scripts/evaluate_platform.py --output site/api/platform-evaluation.json`, which runs every iteration. `scripts/evaluate_platform.py` writes the report with `done` flag, per-item status, traced_cause, and buildability_gaps array (visible in the script's `report = {...}` block including `buildability_gaps` and `runtimePrereqStatus`).

`scripts/codex_loop.py` defines `read_overrides(repo)` that reads `docs/loop-overrides.md`, `overrides_paused(text)` that halts the loop when a `PAUSE` token sits on its own line outside HTML comments (checked BEFORE the next iteration: `if overrides_paused(overrides): print(...); return 0` inside the iteration loop, before any work begins), and `format_overrides_for_prompt` which injects the cleaned overrides under a 'Human overrides from `docs/loop-overrides.md` (AUTHORITATIVE...)' section in the prompt.

`scripts/codex_loop.py` checks `if after_eval and after_eval.get('done'): print('Evaluator reports done after this iteration. Stopping the loop.'); return 0`. Hard caps (max-iterations, stall-limit) remain as safety rails. No numeric threshold gating is used for termination — only the LLM evaluator's `done` flag.

buildability_gaps.gap: No documented curl, SQL, or POST endpoint for creating an organization. `docs/supabase-hosted-database.md` only shows a SELECT smoke test; the migration shows GRANT SELECT but no INSERT path is documented for `organizations`.
buildability_gaps.traced_cause: docs/build-an-edtech-app.md: add a step that POSTs to `https://qzxlgrerjoiamxvnkklq.supabase.co/rest/v1/organizations` with the required tenant-scoped JWT, naming each field via dictionary/oneroster-core.v1.json#organization links.

buildability_gaps.gap: No guide explains how to assemble organization_id, course_id, term_id, and class fields into a POST. Layperson would need to read OneRoster spec to fill in `class_type` enum values.
buildability_gaps.traced_cause: docs/build-an-edtech-app.md plus data/data-dictionary.seed.json shared_enum_id for class_type: write the guided POST and back it with a global enum so the layperson can link to one canonical class_type vocabulary.

buildability_gaps.gap: Edge Function `gradebook-bulk-submit` is documented in `docs/supabase-hosted-database.md` but the layperson must construct a `$SUPABASE_USER_JWT` with a `tenant_id` claim, and no doc explains how to obtain such a JWT or which `scoreStatus` values are valid without reading the dictionary JSON directly.
buildability_gaps.traced_cause: docs/build-an-edtech-app.md: walk through obtaining a tenant-scoped JWT (or sandbox credentials), then post a result, with links into dictionary/oneroster-core.v1.json#result.score_status for the enum.

buildability_gaps.gap: There is no live CASE import/search endpoint (DEC-012 marks CASE as doc-only) and no guide that explains how to attach a CASE URI to a line item. The layperson cannot produce an executable artifact for this step.
buildability_gaps.traced_cause: dictionary/oneroster-core.v1.json#line_item: add a `case_standard_uri` projection or document the attachment via the dictionary alignment graph; then write the corresponding step in docs/build-an-edtech-app.md. This may require advancing PEND-001 to schedule the CASE runtime slice.

buildability_gaps.gap: `caliper-event-ingestion` accepts events but no read-back endpoint is documented; the rubric requires reading a Caliper-style activity feed for the class. The dictionary describes `caliper_event` fields but no GET path exists in evidence.
buildability_gaps.traced_cause: supabase/functions or supabase/migrations: add a `caliper-event-feed` GET endpoint or REST view scoped by class_id and tenant; document it in docs/build-an-edtech-app.md with field links to dictionary/caliper-core.v1.json#caliper_event.

buildability_gaps.gap: Layperson would need to convert between OneRoster `person.email`, Caliper `caliper_actor.identifier_value`, and LTI `lti_membership.email` because no canonical_field_id ties them together. The substance_check explicitly forbids requiring spec conversion.
buildability_gaps.traced_cause: data/data-dictionary.seed.json#person.email plus canonical_field_id projections on the three per-spec email fields: unify identity at the data layer so the guide can reference one canonical email field instead of three spec-native ones.

projections.note: Structural per-decision projection index generated from `projects_to`; semantic contradictions are graded by the projections_match_reality rubric item.

decisions.title: DEC-001 Person, User, Actor, Profile, and Subject
decisions.choice: The platform has a canonical `person` for known school people and a broader `agent` concept for non-person actors. Credential subjects and Caliper actors resolve to `person` only when policy and identifiers allow it; otherwise they remain linked external agents.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-002 Class, Course, Context, Group, Course Section, and Organization
decisions.choice: The platform separates `course` from `class`. `course` is the catalog/template. `class` is the scheduled teaching section. LTI contexts and Caliper groups can map to a class, course, or other learning context. Common Cartridge organizations are content outlines, not school organizations or scheduled classes.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-003 Roles
decisions.choice: Store source roles exactly, then map them to platform role families: `learner`, `educator`, `administrator`, `guardian`, `mentor`, `staff`, `tool`, `issuer`, and `unknown`.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Referenced artifact contains the projection's primary token.

decisions.title: DEC-004 Enrollment and Membership
decisions.choice: `enrollment` is the canonical roster participation record. `membership` is a contextual service/event view that may be derived from enrollment or captured from an external system.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-005 Results, Scores, Outcomes, and Grade Events
decisions.choice: The platform uses `line_item` as the gradebook target and `result` as the learner's current gradebook state. LTI AGS Score is an incoming write/update message. LTI AGS Result and OneRoster Result map to current result state. QTI outcomes are assessment-runtime variables projected into results only when a delivery or scoring workflow declares that mapping. Caliper GradeEvent is event history, not the gradebook source of truth.

decisions.title: DEC-006 Standards Alignment
decisions.choice: CASE is the canonical standards graph. Other standards store alignment as source metadata and resolve known targets to CASE item identifiers or URIs.

decisions.title: DEC-007 Identifiers
decisions.choice: Internal records have platform IDs. Standard-shaped endpoints expose the standard-native ID as the primary contract when required. Every external ID is stored in a source identifier crosswalk with source system, identifier type, and object relationship.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-008 Time, School Sessions, and Event Timestamps
decisions.choice: Separate calendar periods, event timestamps, availability windows, and validity periods. Store timestamps in UTC with source timezone/offset when provided. Store OneRoster AcademicSession as the school calendar backbone.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-009 Content, Resources, Packages, and Launch Links
decisions.choice: The platform uses a broad `resource` concept for discoverable learning objects, with subtype-specific records for cartridge resources, QTI packages/items/tests, LTI resource links, and Caliper event entities.

decisions.title: DEC-010 Tenant-Owned Data and Shared Reference Data
decisions.choice: Tenant-owned operational records carry tenant boundaries and row-level policy. Public CASE frameworks, public standards metadata, public certification fixtures, and optionally public item banks live in shared reference namespaces. Tenants adopt, pin, override, or privately extend shared reference data through explicit records.

projections.detail: Referenced artifact contains the projection's primary token.
Referenced artifact contains the projection's primary token.

decisions.title: DEC-011 Privacy Surfaces
decisions.choice: Generated docs may describe every field and privacy class because they are schema documentation, not records. Live PostgREST may expose tenant-owned `public`, `operational`, `directory`, and `education_record` records only through tenant RLS and scope policy. Edge Functions may expose `directory`, `education_record`, `behavioral`, or sensitive operational fields only through caller JWT, purpose, scope, tenant RLS, and audit logging when the read is sensitive. Static `site/api/*.json` mirrors may contain only synthetic demo records from the seeded `.test` tenant and may include `public`, `operational`, and `directory` fields such as demo email; they may not contain real tenant data, `education_record`, `behavioral`, `sensitive_pii`, or secret/system values.

decisions.title: DEC-012 Runtime Coverage Per Spec
decisions.choice: OneRoster core is runtime-backed now through local SQLite, hosted Supabase PostgREST, RLS, and live tests. QTI and CASE are doc-only/generated dictionary projections until runtime slices are built. Caliper, LTI, Security Framework, and Data Privacy have partial runtime backing through authenticated Edge Function receipt/governance paths, but not full standard conformance flows. Full runtime for QTI import, CASE search, Caliper raw-event projection, LTI Advantage services, production OAuth, and privacy workflows remains future work with target dates in the decision/pending work queue.

decisions.title: DEC-013 Audit Response Truth
decisions.choice: Audited Edge Functions must use truthful responses when they include an `audit` block. If the function cannot read the written `audit_log` rows back under the caller's JWT and request identifier, it must omit the audit block or return an explicit error; it must not hard-code `logged` counts or field lists.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-014 Static Mirror Policy
decisions.choice: Static mirrors are allowed as documentation fixtures for GitHub Pages and offline inspection. They are not the canonical runtime contract; live Supabase REST and Edge Functions are the runtime contract. Mirrors must be generated from the same synthetic demo seed as the local demo, must carry source metadata, and may only include privacy classes allowed by `DEC-011-privacy-surfaces`.

decisions.title: DEC-015 Service-Role Policy
decisions.choice: Request-scoped runtime code and Edge Functions must pass the caller's `Authorization` bearer token to Supabase so RLS applies. Service-role access is allowed only for allowlisted admin or test fixture operations in `docs/admin-operations.md`, with a named operation ID, caller, why-user-JWT-insufficient reason, guardrails, and recent review date. Tests may use service role to create temporary Auth users, but not to read tenant data or satisfy the assertion under test.

decisions.title: DEC-016 Dictionary Generation Direction
decisions.choice: `data/data-dictionary.seed.json` is upstream. `dictionary/*.v1.json` files are generated projections; hand-editing a per-spec projection is a build error once the generator lands.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-017 Canonical Field Reconciliation
decisions.choice: Every overlapping spec field carries `canonical_field_id` pointing at the shared dictionary. Spec-native names remain for conformance; cross-spec app code uses the canonical field identity.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-018 Global Enum Crosswalks
decisions.choice: Allowed values are global dictionary objects. Each enum field references `shared_enum_id`; the shared enum maps every spec-native value to and from a canonical value.

projections.detail: Referenced artifact contains the projection's primary token.

decisions.title: DEC-019 Closed Privacy Classes
decisions.choice: Closed keys are `public`, `operational`, `directory`, `education_record`, `behavioral`, `sensitive`, `credential`, `privacy_governance`, and `system`. `depends_on_entity` and `depends_on_contents` are invalid.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-020 Relational Graph Migrations
decisions.choice: Shared dictionary objects own source field, target object, target field, cardinality, ownership, and delete behavior. Supabase migrations are generated from that graph.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
